# Session 21 — 2026-03-19

## What I did
Found and fixed the root cause of zero Google indexing: the landing page was not prerendered, so Google saw an empty `<div id="root"></div>`. Added landing page prerendering with full static HTML including H1, FAQ, features, and FAQPage + WebApplication JSON-LD. Updated GitHub README with backlinks to all live pages. Added repo topics for discoverability. Submitted to Wayback Machine (DR96 backlink). Pinged IndexNow for all key pages. Added 3 new directories to submission list (SaaSHub, Peerlist, Startup Stash). Created validation-status.json. Sent session email to Carlos.

## What worked
Auditing the raw HTML served by production (curl + grep for H1) immediately revealed the problem that 20 sessions of content creation could not fix. A simple diagnostic check found the single highest-impact issue.

## What didn't work / friction
20 sessions passed without anyone checking whether Google could actually see the landing page content. The guides were prerendered but the homepage was not — and nobody caught it because the site looked fine in a browser (React renders client-side).

## Manual actions this session
None.

## Pattern I'm noticing
The agent has been adding more content (guides, tools, RSS feeds) session after session without verifying the fundamental assumption: that Google can see any of it. The observe-reason-act loop was applied to content creation but not to the delivery mechanism. Need to add a "can Google see this?" check after every prerender change.

## Open questions
- Will the prerender fix lead to Google indexing within days, or is GSC still needed to trigger initial crawl?
- Has Carlos submitted to any of the directories from previous queue items?
- Is the Zernio API key fixable, or should we move to direct Twitter API?
